serving.natural_language_portion: One Croque Monsieur sandwich, approximately 200 g, consisting of two slices of toasted white bread filled with mortadella, mornay sauce, mustard, harissa, and torched melted cheese.

nutrient_source.title: Croque Monsieur — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Bread white (FDC ID 269714), Ham cured (FDC ID 174608), Gruyère cheese (FDC ID 173414), Butter (FDC ID 173410), Whole milk (FDC ID 746782), Wheat flour (FDC ID 169761)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Croque Monsieur as a composite dish. Nutrient values per 100 g were estimated by weighted ingredient composition: white bread (~35%), cured ham/mortadella (~20%), Gruyère-style cheese (~15%), mornay sauce (whole milk, butter, flour, ~20%), mustard and harissa (~5%), butter spread (~5%). Macros cross-checked against published French culinary nutrition references. Values represent a plausible mid-range estimate for a restaurant-style Croque Monsieur.

gi_source.title: White bread sandwich GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Croque Monsieur. GI estimated at 53 based on white bread sandwich analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., 2008). White bread GI is approximately 70–75, but the presence of protein-rich ham and mortadella, fat from cheese and mornay sauce, and the egg-enriched mornay significantly attenuate the glycemic response, consistent with published mixed-meal GI attenuation data. GI of 53 is a conservative mid-range estimate for a protein- and fat-rich hot sandwich on white bread. GL per serving = (53 × 24.5 × 200/100) / 100 = 26.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~720 mg per 100 g; ~1440 mg per serving) from cured meats, mornay sauce, and cheese
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Croque Monsieur due to its very high sodium load (~1440 mg per 200 g serving, representing approximately 60–70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg), primarily contributed by mortadella, cured ham, and processed cheese.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~26 per serving) from refined white bread carbohydrates, partially attenuated by protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Croque Monsieur in moderation; the white bread base contributes a moderate glycemic load (~26 per serving), though the high protein and fat content from cheese and cured meats attenuate the postprandial glucose response, and portion control remains essential.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~7.2 g per 100 g; ~14.4 g per serving) and high sodium from cheese, mornay sauce, butter, and cured meats
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Croque Monsieur consumption due to its high saturated fat content (~14.4 g per serving, exceeding the recommended daily limit of 20 g for many adults) and very high sodium (~1440 mg per serving), both of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Croque Monsieur is a classic French hot sandwich consisting of sliced white bread filled with cured ham and melted cheese, typically Gruyère or Emmental, and topped with a rich béchamel or mornay sauce that is then grilled or baked until golden and bubbling. The name translates loosely as 'Mister Crunch' in French, reflecting the crisp, toasted exterior. This Singapore restaurant version from To;asta features mortadella (an Italian cured pork sausage) in place of traditional French ham, paired with a mustard-infused mornay sauce, harissa (a North African chilli paste) for heat, and torched melted cheese on top, giving it a smoky, caramelised finish. The result is a rich, indulgent sandwich with a crispy bread exterior, creamy and savoury interior, and complex layered flavours from the spiced meats, tangy mustard, and spicy harissa. Nutritionally, Croque Monsieur is energy-dense (~280 kcal per 100 g), high in protein (~13.5 g per 100 g) from the cured meats and cheese, and high in saturated fat (~7.2 g per 100 g) and sodium (~720 mg per 100 g) from the processed meat and dairy components. It carries a moderate glycemic load due to the white bread base, partially attenuated by its fat and protein content. Clinically, it is a high-concern food for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to sodium and saturated fat, and moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes management. It is not suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, or gluten-free diets.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Croque Monsieur as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for white bread, cured ham, Gruyère-style cheese, butter, and mornay sauce ingredients. GI was estimated by analogy from white bread sandwich data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). The Singapore restaurant version uses mortadella and harissa, which are non-standard ingredients that introduce additional uncertainty. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Cuisine correction: The input JSON incorrectly tagged this dish as 'Japanese' with cuisine_tags ['Japanese', 'Pasta', 'Udon']. Croque Monsieur is a French dish; cuisine has been corrected to 'French' and region_of_origin to 'France'. The field survey restaurant tags appear to be erroneous platform metadata. 2. Ingredient note: This version uses mortadella (Italian cured pork) rather than traditional French jambon, and adds harissa and mustard mornay, making it a fusion variant. 3. Nutrient estimation: All per_100g values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data; no direct composite entry exists. Values represent a plausible mid-range estimate. 4. Sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g (4.9), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.4), trans_fat_g (0.2), potassium_mg (210.0), calcium_mg (185.0), iron_mg (1.8), cholesterol_mg (58.0), vitamin_c_mg (1.0), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (72.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.18), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.22), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (2.8), vitamin_b6_mg (0.18), vitamin_b12_mcg (0.6), folate_mcg_dfe (38.0), vitamin_d_mcg (0.4), vitamin_e_mg (0.8), phosphorus_mg (220.0), magnesium_mg (22.0), zinc_mg (1.6), selenium_mcg (14.0), water_g (42.0) are all estimated from USDA component data and are not -1 sentinel values; they are positive estimates. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record. 5. Multilingual names: Croque Monsieur is a French dish; no mandatory non-English local-language names are required by the Veritas multilingual matrix (which mandates additional languages only for Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese dishes). 6. Halal status: Mortadella and cured ham are pork products; this dish is not halal. 7. GI category: GI = 53, which is ≤ 55, therefore gi_category = 'low' per Veritas threshold rules.
